issues.message: The map quality report caps confidence at medium.

notes: This report evaluates whether the current map exposes decision-support ingredients; it does not judge whether the underlying sources are complete.
Missing slots should be acknowledged in the briefing when they matter to the question, not filled in by inference.

default_population.claim: In the pooled multivariable analysis, consumption of at least one egg per day was not associated with incident cardiovascular disease risk after adjustment for updated lifestyle and dietary factors.

default_population.value: In the pooled multivariable analysis, consumption of at least one egg per day was not associated with incident cardiovascular disease...

default_population.claim: High egg consumption was associated with a higher risk of cardiovascular disease in people with type 2 diabetes.
default_population.source: Egg consumption and risk of cardiovascular disease: three large prospective US cohort studies, systematic review, and updated meta-analysis
default_population.value: High egg consumption was associated with a higher risk of cardiovascular disease in people with type 2 diabetes.

default_population.claim: Each additional 300 mg of dietary cholesterol consumed per day was significantly associated with a higher relative risk and absolute risk of incident CVD and all-cause mortality.

default_population.value: Each additional 300 mg of dietary cholesterol consumed per day was significantly associated with a higher relative risk and absolute...

default_population.source: Here's the latest on dietary cholesterol and how it fits in with a healthy diet
default_population.value: Anyone with a high LDL cholesterol level should consider reducing sources of both saturated fat and dietary cholesterol.

default_population.value: free of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and cancer at baseline

setting_or_context.claim: For intakes of <3 and ≥3 eggs/week, OR = 1.14 (95% CI 1.07–1.22) and OR = 1.25 (95% CI 1.14–1.38), respectively. For intakes of <3, 3–5, and ≥5 eggs/week, OR = 1.13 (95% CI 1.06–1.21), OR = 1.14 (95% CI 1.01–1.29), and OR = 1.19 (95% CI 1.01–1.39), respectively. In site-specific analyses, egg intake was associated mainly with risk of cancers of the stomach, colon, and colorectum. Increased risk was found only in case–control studies. No associations were found in European studies.
setting_or_context.source: Eggs - a scoping review for Nordic Nutrition Recommendations 2023
setting_or_context.value: respectively. In site-specific analyses, egg intake was associated mainly with risk of cancers of the stomach, colon, and
